questions.question: In a practical OFDM system with N-point FFT and sampling rate f_s, what condition ensures orthogonality among subcarriers?

answers.a: Subcarrier spacing equals the baud rate
answers.d: Cyclic prefix duration equals one symbol duration

explanations.a: Incorrect! Baud rate relates to symbol transmission, not subcarrier orthogonality.
explanations.b: Correct! In digital OFDM, subcarrier spacing = f_s / N ensures orthogonality.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Amplitude differences do not guarantee orthogonality.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Cyclic prefix prevents ISI but does not ensure orthogonality.

questions.question: Which impairments in practical OFDM systems can cause loss of subcarrier orthogonality?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Phase noise disturbs subcarrier phases but is not the only cause of orthogonality loss.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Frequency offsets lead to inter-carrier interference, but other factors also contribute.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Nonlinear amplification distorts subcarrier amplitudes and phases but alone does not explain all orthogonality loss.
explanations.d: Correct! Phase noise, frequency offsets, and nonlinearities all degrade orthogonality and cause ICI.

questions.question: What is the main disadvantage of using a long cyclic prefix in OFDM?

answers.a: It increases the peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR)
answers.b: It reduces the bit error rate (BER)
answers.d: It decreases the effective data rate

explanations.a: Incorrect. PAPR is not directly affected by cyclic prefix length.
explanations.b: Incorrect. While a longer cyclic prefix can slightly improve BER, it comes at a cost.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Longer cyclic prefix reduces spectral efficiency, not increases it.
explanations.d: Correct! A longer cyclic prefix adds overhead, reducing the fraction of time used for transmitting data.

questions.question: To avoid inter-symbol interference (ISI) in OFDM, what must be ensured regarding the guard interval?

answers.a: Symbol duration must be longer than the channel delay spread
answers.b: Guard interval (cyclic prefix) must be longer than the maximum channel delay spread
answers.c: IFFT size must be equal to FFT size
answers.d: Subcarrier spacing must exceed coherence bandwidth

explanations.a: Incorrect. Symbol duration alone is insufficient without a proper cyclic prefix.
explanations.b: Correct! The cyclic prefix should cover the maximum multipath delay to prevent ISI.
explanations.c: Incorrect. FFT/IFFT size is a system design choice but doesn’t guarantee ISI-free transmission.
explanations.d: Incorrect. This condition relates to flat fading per subcarrier, not ISI prevention.

questions.question: Why is OFDM sensitive to frequency synchronization errors?

answers.a: OFDM uses a single carrier for data transmission
answers.b: Orthogonality of subcarriers breaks down with frequency shifts
answers.d: FFT is performed before the cyclic prefix is removed

explanations.a: Incorrect. OFDM is a multi-carrier system.
explanations.b: Correct! Even small frequency offsets introduce inter-carrier interference (ICI), breaking orthogonality.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Guard intervals prevent ISI but do not cause orthogonality issues.
explanations.d: Incorrect. FFT is performed after removing the cyclic prefix.

questions.question: In an OFDM system with 1024 subcarriers, what is the purpose of zero-padding unused subcarriers?

answers.c: Reduces spectral leakage and controls bandwidth

explanations.a: Incorrect. Zero-padding does not significantly affect PAPR.
explanations.b: Incorrect. It does not increase transmitted energy.
explanations.c: Correct! Zero-padding smooths the edges of the spectrum, reducing spectral leakage and defining bandwidth.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Timing is mainly handled by cyclic prefix and synchronization sequences.

questions.question: Why does OFDM inherently have a high peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR)?

answers.b: Because multiple subcarriers can constructively interfere
answers.c: Because the cyclic prefix increases signal power

explanations.a: Incorrect. OFDM uses amplitude-varying modulation (e.g., QAM), not constant envelope.
explanations.b: Correct! When many subcarriers align in phase, large peaks occur in the time-domain signal, causing high PAPR.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The cyclic prefix does not increase peak amplitude.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Filtering may reduce out-of-band leakage but doesn’t solve PAPR.

questions.question: What happens if the cyclic prefix is shorter than the channel’s maximum delay spread?

answers.b: It causes inter-symbol and inter-carrier interference

explanations.a: Incorrect. Frequency diversity is unaffected by cyclic prefix length.
explanations.b: Correct! Insufficient cyclic prefix leads to ISI and ICI, degrading performance.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Latency may reduce slightly but at a major cost in reliability.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Modulation order is independent of cyclic prefix length.

questions.question: In an OFDM system with fixed bandwidth, what is the effect of increasing the FFT size?

answers.a: Improves time resolution and increases ICI
answers.b: Reduces subcarrier spacing and improves frequency resolution
answers.d: Improves spectral efficiency by reducing symbol spacing

explanations.a: Incorrect. Increasing FFT size lengthens the symbol duration, reducing time resolution.
explanations.b: Correct! More FFT points reduce subcarrier spacing (Δf = BW/N), improving frequency resolution but increasing Doppler sensitivity.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Cyclic prefix ratio may increase if its duration is fixed.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Spectral efficiency remains unchanged unless the symbol rate is adjusted.

questions.question: Why is equalization in OFDM often simpler than in single-carrier frequency-selective channels?

answers.a: Each subcarrier experiences flat fading, allowing single-tap equalization
answers.c: The cyclic prefix eliminates the need for equalization

explanations.a: Correct! Each subcarrier sees a flat-fading channel, so one complex gain per subcarrier suffices for equalization.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Multipath still exists; OFDM simplifies its impact per subcarrier.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The cyclic prefix prevents ISI but does not replace equalization for amplitude/phase distortions.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Time-domain diversity is not the main reason equalization is simpler.
